key_points: Law and social justice are closely connected because laws are created to ensure fairness and protect the rights of all citizens
Social justice focuses on reducing inequality and preventing exploitation of weaker sections of society
Labour laws regulate working hours, wages and safety conditions in workplaces
Governments create regulations to make sure industries follow environmental and safety standards
Laws also protect workers from hazardous working conditions and ensure compensation in case of accidents
Courts and government authorities enforce these laws to maintain justice and accountability
examples: The Bhopal Gas Tragedy highlighted the need for strict industrial safety laws
Minimum wage laws ensure workers are paid fairly for their labour
Environmental laws prevent companies from polluting air and water
Courts can order companies to compensate victims when laws are violated
formulas: Social Justice → Equality + Protection of rights + Fair opportunities
Labour Protection → Minimum wage + Safety laws + Working hour regulations
Industrial Responsibility → Law compliance + Environmental protection
exam_tips: Explain how law helps achieve social justice in society
Use examples like industrial safety laws or labour protection laws
Mention the role of courts and government in enforcing laws
Include terms such as exploitation, equality, labour rights and environmental protection
